todaysPlan.activity: Homework Spelling – Silent Letters And High-Frequency Words
todaysPlan.reason: Active uploaded homework is "Benchmark Advance Spelling Unit 9 Week 3", so today's required work must anchor on silent letter patterns (kn, gn, wr), high-frequency sight word recognition, orthographic memory before broader profile goals.
todaysPlan.method: Read the assignment-aligned content, ask evidence-based checks, then use profile motivators only as framing.
probeSequence: Read or preview the assignment content
Ask why it happened using text evidence
Have the child explain the concept back

childProfile: Reina is an 8-year-old championship-level learner who thrives on competition, speed, and being pushed hard. She's academically a year ahead, bilingual (English + Japanese), and a competitive wrestler on an 8-year-old girls team. She learns best through challenge that matches her identity: wrestling metaphors (takedowns, pins, championships), competitive framing (streaks, scoreboards, personal bests), and fast-paced work that respects her intelligence. She celebrates in Japanese (すごい, やった) when she succeeds and self-regulates beautifully—exits on winning streaks, debugs technical glitches analytically, and advocates clearly for what she needs. Her 15-20 minute focus window is consistent; respect it by front-loading the hardest work (multiplication) first. She asked for multiplication/division help 79 days ago and deserves to be heard. Easy work makes her disengage; appropriately hard work makes her light up. She's not just smart—she's a strategic thinker who sees patterns, catches errors, and wants to be challenged like the champion she is.
stopAfter: After the active homework check is complete, or earlier if frustration is rising.
rewardPolicy: Reward only after the active homework is checked; broader review goals belong in review mode.
